MASTER SERVICE AGREEMENT 
 
This Master Service Agreement (this “Agreement”) is made at New Delhi on this 13th day of November, 
2017 (the “Execution Date”) among:  
(1) 
ATC TELECOM INFRASTRUCTURE PRIVATE LIMITED, a company incorporated under the laws of 
India under the Companies Act, 1956 and whose registered office is at 403, 4th Floor, Skyline 
Icon, Andheri Kurla Road, Near Mittal Industrial Estate, Andheri (East), Mumbai, Maharashtra, 
India, 400059, India (the “PI company”, which expression shall, unless the context requires 
otherwise, mean and include its successors-in-interest and permitted assigns);  
(2) 
VODAFONE INDIA LIMITED, a company incorporated under the laws of India under the 
Companies Act, 1956 and whose registered office is at Peninsula Corporate Park, Ganpatrao 
Kadam Marg, Lower Parel, Mumbai 400 013, India (“VIL”, which expression shall, unless the 
context requires otherwise, mean and include its successors-in-interest and permitted assigns); 
and 
(3) 
VODAFONE MOBILE SERVICES LIMITED, a company incorporated under the laws of India under 
the Companies Act, 1956 and whose registered office is at Peninsula Corporate Park, Ganpatrao 
Kadam Marg, Lower Parel, Mumbai 400 013, India (“VMSL”, which expression shall, unless the 
context requires otherwise, mean and include its successors-in-interest and permitted assigns).  
VIL and VMSL shall be referred to individually as the “Sharing Operator” and collectively as the “Sharing 
Operators”.  The PI company, VIL and VMSL are individually referred to as a “Party” and collectively as 
the “Parties”. 
 
Background: 
A. 
The PI company is registered with the DoT (as defined below) for providing passive infrastructure 
services and owns or has acquired and possesses, or will acquire and possess, certain 
telecommunications sites, infrastructure and equipment in various licensed Circles in India and is 
engaged, inter alia, in providing Passive Infrastructure (as defined below) and related operations 
and maintenance services to various telecommunications operators in India on a shared basis. 
B. 
In order to ensure operational efficiency and optimize costs, each Sharing Operator wishes to 
access certain Passive Infrastructure provided by the PI company, to the extent permitted by 
applicable laws in India. 
C. 
The PI company has represented to each Sharing Operator that it owns and operates the Passive 
Infrastructure required to meet the requirement of such Sharing Operator and is willing to offer 
its Passive Infrastructure on a sharing basis to the extent permitted by applicable laws in India 
and, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

2.1.4 
In the event that the Service Orders received by the PI company from the Sharing 
Operator in respect of any Site(s) mean that the available Passive Infrastructure at such 
Site(s) are over-subscribed, an applicant whose service order was received by the PI 
company prior to another service order shall be given priority by PI company while 
allocating such Passive Infrastructure to the relevant applicants.

Availability, the relevant Parties shall execute a Service Contract in accordance with the

to the Sharing Operator on a monthly basis.

Site Access Service Credits 
2.3.1 
If the PI company fails to provide the Site Access Availability in accordance with the Site 
Access Service Levels then Site Access Service Credits shall become payable in 
accordance with Schedule 1 (Site Access Availability). The Sharing Operator may, on a 
monthly basis and with a notice of 30 (thirty) days, deduct any such Site Access Service 
Credits from the next invoice due under this Agreement or, if no further invoices are 
due, may recover the Site Access Service Credits as a debt from the PI company due and 
payable within 30 (thirty) days of demand. Any deduction of Site Access Service Credits 
by the Sharing Operator shall be made within the period of six (6) months from the date 
the Sharing Operator is entitled to make such a deduction under the notice, provided 
that, the PI company shall cooperate with the Sharing Operator for the 
resolution/settlement of the dispute during such period and provide a credit note in 
favour of the Sharing Operator within two (2) months from the date of notice. 
2.3.2 
The Parties agree that the Site Access Service Credits are an estimate of the adjustment

2.4 
Service Failures 
2.4.1 
Without prejudice to any other obligations of the PI company under this Agreement, the 
PI company shall promptly warn the Sharing Operator whenever it has reasonable 
grounds to believe that any development (including failure on the part of the PI 
company to carry out its obligations and responsibilities under this Agreement) will 
have, or threatens to have, a detrimental effect on the Site Access Availability. The PI 
company shall take all reasonable steps to prevent such development from occurring 
and to prevent its reoccurrence.  
2.5 
Non-Exclusive 
The relationship between the Parties is on a non-exclusive basis.  The PI company retains the 
right to provide Site Access Availability to other telecommunications operators and the Sharing 
Operator retains the right to seek passive infrastructure services from other passive 
infrastructure providers and telecommunication operators. 
2.6 
Approvals 
2.6.1 
The PI company shall be responsible for obtaining of all necessary and requisite Permits 
for the installation of any Passive Infrastructure, including, without limitation, municipal 
and local bodies approvals, electricity board approvals and pollution control board 
approvals.  
2.6.2 
The Sharing Operator shall be responsible for obtaining Standing Advisory Committee for 
Frequency Allocation (“SACFA”), Wireless Planning Commission (“WPC”) clearance and 
such other Permits from DoT or Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of India or 
other applicable regulatory or statutory body as may be required for the installation of 
Active Infrastructure at a Site from time to time.  
2.6.3 
Support in relation to EMF obligations of Sharing Operator  
(i) 
In accordance with government and regulatory requirements, the PI company 
shall provide signage boards (including “Danger” and “Caution” signages) at the 
Sites in accordance with the specifications set by DoT subject to the terms set 
out in Schedule 3 (Charges). 
(ii) 
At Sites where the EMF radiation level is more than the thresholds prescribed by 
the DOT, the PI Company shall ensure that the sharing operator (including the 
Sharing Operator) who has made the last change due to which the EMF 
radiation levels exceeded the threshold undertakes measures to reduce the 
levels to match the DoT specified thresholds. The PI Company will be 
responsible for intimating all the sharing operators (including the Sharing 
Operator) on that site about the change and facilitate the reduction of EMF 
radiation levels to match the DOT specified thresholds. 
(iii) 
The obligations under this Clause 2.6.3 shall be effective from the Management 
Trigger Date for Existing Sites, and from the Charge Effective Date for New Sites

Provision of Operation and Maintenance Services

The PI company shall provide Operation and Maintenance Services in relation to each

access to the Sharing Operator at such Site, including any premature termination 
of such contract; or  
(c) 
for any other justifiable reason, provided that the PI company is acting 
reasonably and in good faith. 
In case of any Relocation, the PI company shall give at least 45 (forty five) days’ prior 
notice to the Sharing Operator of such Relocation requirement. Additionally, the PI 
company may request a Relocation pursuant to (c) above only with the prior written 
consent of the Sharing Operator.  
In the event of a Relocation, the applicable Service Contract shall, following the removal 
of the last of the Sharing Operator Equipment from the original Site, immediately 
terminate and the relevant Parties shall simultaneously execute a new Service Contract 
in relation to the alternative Site and the PI company shall ensure that there is no 
disruption in the Sharing Operator’s operations which may adversely impact the services 
provided to its subscribers. The terms of the new Service Contract shall be determined in 
accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, provided that: 
(i) 
the term of the new Service Contract shall be the remaining term of the original 
Service Contract;  
(ii) 
the Sharing Operator shall not be subject to Charges greater than the Charges 
applicable under the original Service Contract;  
(iii) 
the other provisions of the new Service Contract shall be materially equivalent 
to those of the original Service Contract; and  
4.2.2 
The Sharing Operator shall be entitled to refuse Relocation if the alternative Site is not to 
its reasonable technical satisfaction or if the terms of the new Service Contract do not 
comply with sub-clause (i), (ii) or (iii) of Clause 4.2.1 above.  If the Sharing Operator 
refuses Relocation, the Service Contract in respect of the existing Site shall be deemed to 
terminate upon the expiration of the 45 (forty five) day notice period or as otherwise 
specified in Clause 4.2.1 above and the Sharing Operator shall, in each case, remove all 
Sharing Operator Equipment at the Site within 15 (fifteen) days of such termination, or 
such other time period as the Parties, acting reasonably, may agree.  If the Sharing 
Operator fails to remove the Sharing Operator Equipment within such time period, the 
PI company shall not be responsible in any manner whatsoever for the Sharing Operator 
Equipment at such Site.  
4.2.3 
If the Sharing Operator refuses Relocation, where the alternative Site is not to its 
reasonable technical satisfaction or where the terms of the new Service Contract do not 
comply with sub-clause (i), (ii) or (iii) of Clause 4.2.1 above, on termination of the 
original Service Contract no Exit Amount shall be payable by the Sharing Operator.  
4.2.4 
The PI company shall be liable to pay the Relocation Fees (without any deductions) to 
the Sharing Operator in the following cases: 
(i) 
any Relocation under Clause 4.2.1  of this Agreement; and 
(ii) 
termination of a Service Contract pursuant to Clause 4.2.2 of this Agreement.

3.1. 
The Sharing Operator shall deliver requests for providing a New Site/New Build Site or upgrading 
of an Existing Site/New Site/New Build Site to the PI company, (“Site Requests”).  Within three 
(3) weeks of receiving a Site Request, the PI company shall respond to the Sharing Operator 
providing comments and/or suggestions with respect to the provision of New Build Site/New 
Site or upgrading the Passive Infrastructure at an Existing Site/New Site/New Build Site, as well 
as an outline of the PI company’s implementation plan and an indication of the possible rental 
costs at such Sites (the “Site Proposal”).  The Site Proposal shall be in the form specified in 
Schedule 4.  
3.2. 
Within two (2) weeks of receiving the Site Proposal relating to a Site Request, the Sharing 
Operator shall issue a Service Order for a New Site/New Build Site or upgrading of an Existing 
Site/New Site/New Build Site in the form specified in Schedule 4.  Where the Sharing Operator 
does not issue a Service Order within two (2) weeks of receiving PI company’s Site Proposal, the 
Sharing Operator will be required to issue a new Site Request.   
3.3. 
A Service Order shall provide the details of the Site and shall also include the technical 
requirements for the Active Infrastructure the Sharing Operator proposes for such Site in 
accordance with the format provided in Schedule 4.  The Service Order shall also contain, Sites 
covered by paragraph 2.2.2 of Schedule 3 (Charges), expected RFAI date, an estimate of the rent 
that the Sharing Operator believes will be payable in respect of the Site, subject to a permissible 
5% variation. In cases above 5% variation, the PI company shall intimate the variation and based 
on that the Sharing Operator shall issue a new Service Order. 
3.4. 
The PI company shall confirm in writing to the Sharing Operator whether it accepts or declines a 
Service Order within one (1) week of receipt of the Service Order. Where PI company accepts a 
Service Order (“PI company Confirmation”), it shall use its best endeavours to provide a New 
Site/New Build Site or upgrade an Existing Site/New Site/New Build Site, in accordance with the 
Acquisition Lead Time set out in paragraph 3.5 below and also indicate expected RFAI date. If 
the PI company does not provide a PI company Confirmation within the one (1) week period, the 
PI company shall have deemed to have provided the PI company Confirmation. 
3.5. 
For the purposes of this Schedule, “Acquisition Lead Time” means the period commencing on 
the date of receipt of the PI company Confirmation and ending on the date of execution of an 
agreement or completion of any other arrangement, as necessary, between the PI company and 
the respective landlord in respect of such Site (“Site Acquisition”).  Subject to paragraph 3.6 
below, the Site Access Service Level relating to Acquisition Lead Time for provision of a New Site 
or acquisition of a New Build Site or upgrading of an Existing Site/New Site/New Build Site shall 
be 15 (fifteen) days. The PI company shall promptly report any delays in the Acquisition Lead 
Time or any variation in the rent from any estimate in the Site Proposal and/or Service Order to 
the Sharing Operator and to the relevant Relationship Managers.  
3.6. 
If the PI company, at any time, despite its best efforts, believes that it will be unable to provide a 
Site in compliance with the Acquisition Lead Time set out in paragraph 3.5 above because of the 
difficult physical location of the proposed Site or any other reason, it shall inform the Sharing 
Operator in writing of such reasons as soon as possible and such Site shall be deemed to be a 
“Difficult Site”. The Acquisition Lead Time for any Difficult Site shall be as mutually agreed 
between the relevant Parties. The number of Difficult Sites cannot be more than 10% of the 
Sharing Operator’s quarterly requirement of New Sites or New Build Sites.

3.7. 
The “Deployment Lead Time” for the deployment of a New Site or New Build Site or upgrading 
an Existing Site is the period commencing on the date of the Site Acquisition and ending on the 
date on which the PI company delivers a “Ready for Active Installation” or “RFAI” notice to the 
Sharing Operator confirming Site Access Availability in respect of such Site.  
Along with the RFAI notice, the PI company shall provide a letter to the Sharing Operator 
confirming the Sharing Operator’s access to the Site.  The format for the RFAI notice to be issued 
by the PI company in respect of each Site is set out at Appendix 1 to this Schedule.  
3.8. 
Prior to the PI company issuing the RFAI notice in respect of a Site, the Sharing Operator may 
choose to withdraw the Service Order relating to such Site provided that, if the Sharing Operator 
is to be the sole operator at such Site, the Sharing Operator shall pay to the PI company all costs 
incurred and/or committed to by the PI company in respect of such Site with a mark-up of 20%.  
If the Sharing Operator is not the sole operator in respect of such Site, the Sharing Operator 
shall be required to pay an amount of INR 100,000 (Rupees One hundred thousand) to the PI 
company.  
3.9. 
The Site Access Service Level in respect of the Deployment Lead Time for each type of Site shall 
be as set out below: 
(i) 
New Build Site consisting of a ground based tower of up to 50 meters and where no 
existing infrastructure is available, 45 (forty five) days; 
(ii) 
New Build Site consisting of a ground based tower higher than 50 meters and where no 
existing infrastructure is available, 50 (fifty) days; 
(iii) 
New Build Site consisting of a roof top tower or any other type of site (other than 
ground based tower), in each case, where no existing infrastructure is available, 25 
(twenty five) days; 
(iv) 
Existing Site or a New Site where existing infrastructure is sufficient to accommodate 
expansion, seven (7) days;  
(v) 
Existing Site or a New Site where the existing infrastructure needs to be upgraded to 
meet the Sharing Operator’s Standard Configuration set out in paragraph 1 above, 21 
(twenty one) days; 
(vi) 
Existing Site or a New Site where the existing infrastructure needs to be upgraded to 
meet technical requirements of the Sharing Operator that are in addition to the 
standard configuration set out in paragraph 1 above, as agreed between the Parties on a 
case-by-case basis; 
(vii) 
Difficult Sites, as defined in paragraph 3.6 above, as agreed between the Parties on a 
case-by-case basis.  
3.10. 
The Sharing Operator shall inspect the Site within 14 (fourteen) days of receipt of the RFAI 
notice (the “Inspection Period”) and convey its acceptance of the Site to PI company or notify to 
PI company any defects in the Site.  If the Sharing Operator fails to respond to an RFAI notice 
within the Inspection Period, the concerned Site shall be deemed to have been accepted by the 
Sharing Operator upon the expiration of the Inspection Period.

3.11. 
If the Sharing Operator has notified the PI company of any Material Defects in the Site within 
the first Inspection Period, it may require such Material Defects to be rectified by the PI 
company to the satisfaction of the Sharing Operator within seven (7) days of notification to the 
PI company. The PI company shall issue a second RFAI notice after rectification of material 
defects brought to its notice by the Sharing Operator and the Sharing Operator shall convey its 
acceptance of the Site to the PI company or notify the PI company of any defect in the Site 
within seven (7) days of giving the second RFAI notice (the “Second Inspection Period”).  If the 
Sharing Operator fails to respond to the second RFAI notice within Second Inspection Period, the 
concerned Site shall be deemed to have been accepted by the Sharing Operator upon the 
expiration of such Second Inspection Period. For the purposes of this Schedule, “Material 
Defects” means any failure of the PI company to achieve or comply with the Sharing Operator’s 
technical specifications as set out in a Service Order or any other defect attributable to the PI 
company that prevents the Sharing Operator from commencing the installation of its Active 
Infrastructure in respect of a Site, provided that the Sharing Operator is responsible for the fine 
tuning of the orientation and verticality of the antennae poles during installation of the Sharing 
Operator Equipment at each Site.    
3.12. 
If the Sharing Operator fails to respond to a RFAI notice within the first Inspection Period or 
Second Inspection Period, no Site Access Service Credits shall be payable by the PI company.  
Provided, however, that the Sharing Operator may require the PI company to rectify any 
Material Defects subsequent to the Inspection Period which prevent the Sharing Operator from 
utilising the Site within seven (7) days of PI company being notified of such Material Defects. For 
the avoidance of doubt, the Sharing Operator shall not be required to pay any Charges for any 
period of delay beyond seven (7) days, on a pro rata basis.   
3.13. 
If the Sharing Operator notifies the PI company of any defects in the Site that are not Material 
Defects, either within the Inspection Period or subsequent thereto, it may require such defects 
to be rectified by the PI company within 30 (thirty) days of notice of these defects being given to 
the PI company.

Schedule 2 
Operation and Maintenance Services 
1. 
Uptime  
1.1. 
For the purposes of this Schedule: (a) “Uptime” means the time during which the Sharing 
Operator’s network is operational, as measured at the Sharing Operator’s Network Operations 
Centre (“NOC”), and at the PI company’s Tower Operations Centre (“TOC”), once the PI 
company’s TOC becomes operational; and (b) “Downtime” means the time during which Sharing 
Operator’s network is not operational.  It is further clarified that (i) any Downtime that is 
attributable to the Passive Infrastructure or the activities of any other operator at a Site shall be 
to the account of the PI company (“PI company Downtime”), and (ii) any Downtime that is 
attributable to the Sharing Operator Equipment shall be to the account of the Sharing Operator 
(“Sharing Operator Downtime”).    
1.2. 
The applicable Operation and Maintenance Service Level (the “Uptime Service Level”) shall 
measure the Uptime after taking into account any Sharing Operator Downtime to the extent 
such Sharing Operator Downtime is not included in the calculation of the PI company Downtime 
in accordance with paragraph 1.8 below.   
1.3. 
The Uptime Service Level shall be calculated on a monthly basis for each Circle across all Sites 
utilised by the Sharing Operator in that Circle at the beginning of each month. 
1.4. 
For purposes of calculating the Uptime in relation to a Site, any planned PI company Downtime 
for repairs and maintenance and any Sharing Operator Downtime shall be excluded.  Where the 
PI company proposes to conduct any repairs and maintenance at a Site that may cause PI 
company Downtime, PI company shall submit a written request to the Sharing Operator in 
respect of such planned PI company Downtime.  The Sharing Operator shall respond in writing 
to such request within two (2) Business Days granting or refusing permission to the PI company. 
The Sharing Operator may withhold permission where it believes that it is commercially 
reasonable for the PI company to conduct the proposed repairs and maintenance without any PI 
company Downtime. Where the Sharing Operator does not respond within such time period, 
permission shall be deemed to be granted to the PI company.  The Sharing Operator can refuse 
the PI company’s request maximum of two (2) times in a month for the same Site beyond which 
it shall be deemed acceptable. The PI company shall, on a best efforts basis, schedule such 
planned PI company Downtime for a time convenient to all operators at the Site.  If the 
operators are unable to agree on a convenient time for such PI company Downtime, the PI 
company shall plan such PI company Downtimes between 1:00 am and 5:00 am.  Prior to the 
commencement of any planned PI company Downtime, PI company shall inform the Sharing 
Operator’s NOC of such planned PI company Downtime, at least two (2) hours before 
commencement. 
1.5. 
The PI company shall not plan PI company Downtime greater than four (4) hours in aggregate in 
a calendar month at a particular Site.  Where any planned PI company Downtime exceeds four 
(4) hours in aggregate in a calendar month at a particular Site, all subsequent PI company 
Downtime at such Site shall be treated as unplanned PI company Downtime, unless such 
subsequent PI company Downtime at the Site is with the prior written consent of the Sharing 
Operator.

1.6. 
Any PI company Downtime that may be caused by the planned activities of any other operator 
at a Site shall be notified to the Sharing Operator at least 24 (twenty four) hours prior to the 
commencement of such activities.   
1.7. 
Where any activities of the Sharing Operator result in any downtime that requires the PI 
company to make any payments to any other operator(s) at the Site, the PI company shall be 
entitled to recover an amount equal to the aggregate of such payments from the Sharing 
Operator.  Provided that nothing in this paragraph shall require the Sharing Operator to 
reimburse the PI company for any payments made to other operator(s) at the Site in excess of 
the Operation and Maintenance Service Credits that would have been payable by the PI 
company to the Sharing Operator in respect of such downtime if it was PI company Downtime, 
multiplied by the number of other operators at the Site. 
1.8. 
The PI company shall ensure proper access to the Sites for the authorised personnel of the 
Sharing Operator for the purposes set out in Clause 3.1.2 of the Agreement.  For this purpose, 
the Sharing Operator shall notify the PI company in writing of such requirement, provided that 
such notification shall not be required in case any maintenance activities are required to be 
carried out to remedy any faults relating to the Sharing Operator Equipment in the Site. 
1.9. 
Any Sharing Operator Downtime in relation to a Site on account of non-access to such Site that 
is attributable to the PI company shall also be included in the calculation of PI company 
Downtime. For the purposes of determining such PI company Downtime on account of the 
Sharing Operator’s non-access to a Site, the PI company Downtime shall be calculated 
commencing from the expiration of: 
(i) 
30 (thirty) minutes in case of Sites located in the top 83 cities (set out at Appendix 2); and   
(ii) 
one (1) hour for all other Sites,  
subsequent to the Sharing Operator having informed PI company of such non-access, until such 
time as access is provided.   
However, in the event that the access to any Site is impeded by any natural causes such as 
inclement weather or for reasons beyond the control of the PI company, the delay in 
provisioning of access shall not be calculated towards the PI company Downtime.  However, 
similar DC uptime will be provided to Sharing Operator Equipment as it would be available to PI 
company’s equipment. 
The Sharing Operator shall ensure that its activities at the Site do not cause any inconvenience 
to the landlord or neighbours at such Site.  Any non-access to a Site that is attributable to any 
such inconvenience shall not be counted towards PI company Downtime under this paragraph.  
1.10. 
Any recurring incidents of non-access shall be monitored and discussed by the Relationship 
Managers, and the PI company shall use its best endeavours to rectify such recurring incidents, 
within one (1) month of such discussion by the Relationship Managers. 
1.11. 
The expected Uptime Service Level in each Circle for each month is 99.95% across all Sites in 
that Circle that are utilised by the Sharing Operator except the Sites in Jammu & Kashmir, Bihar, 
Assam and North-East Circles where it will be 99.9%.

Schedule 3 
Charges 
1. 
Introduction 
The Charges payable by the Sharing Operator in respect of each Site provided by the PI company 
to the Sharing Operator shall be determined in accordance with this Schedule 3 and shall be 
applicable from the Charge Effective Date unless expressly stated otherwise. For the avoidance 
of doubt, if the introduction of any additional technology including but not limited to 3G, Wi-
Max, Wi-Fi, 4G, LTE or any further evolution of the technology by the Sharing Operator at a Site 
where such Sharing Operator is already utilizing the Passive Infrastructure results in any loading 
in accordance with the provisions of this Schedule, additional charges shall be payable only to 
the extent of such loading. 
It is further agreed that this Schedule 3 specifies the Charges payable by the Sharing Operator 
with respect to such Sites where the Sharing Operator has installed or proposes to install only 
such Active Infrastructure that supports the operation of the Sharing Operator. 
For the avoidance of doubt, it is clarified that the Sharing Operator shall be entitled to 
undertake active infrastructure sharing (RAN sharing) to the extent of:  
(a)  5% of the total portfolio of the Existing Sites; and  
(b)  5% of the New Build Sites taken in a rural area in any Financial Year (without carry forward). 
2. 
Applicable Charges 
2.1

The following rate card (the “Standard Rate”) specifies the amount payable by the Sharing 
Operator at a Site per month and shall be applicable in relation to the Sharing Operator’s

(i) 
applying the formula mentioned in above paragraph 2.2.2 for arriving the amount based on

(ii)

above), P shall be deemed to be equal to F. For the avoidance of doubt, the Base Rate 
payable by the existing sharing operator(s) after further sharing of a Site as mentioned above 
shall not be more than the Base Rate payable by the existing sharing operator(s) prior to 
further sharing of such Site. 
In the event, the rent paid by the PI company to the landlord of a Site utilized by the Sharing 
Operator decreases for any reason, the Standard Rate payable by the Sharing Operator, as 
adjusted in accordance with paragraphs 2.2.1 and 2.2.2 above, shall be decreased by the “Rent 
Decrease Amount”. 
The Rent Decrease Amount shall be (0.8 x (P - D))/T, where “P” is the actual rent paid by the PI 
company to the landlord of the Site prior to the decrease in rent and “D” is the actual rent 
payable by the PI company to the landlord of the Site after the decrease in rent, provided 
further that if D is less than F (as defined in paragraph 2.2.2 above), D shall be deemed to be 
equal to F; and T is the number of sharing operator(s) at such Site. 
High Deposit Sites 
In respect of Sites, where Security Deposit/ Advance Lease Rent/ Advance License Fee/ Premium 
e.g., in Defence Land, in relation to the land/premises at which such Site is located is non-refundable, 
the Standard Rates set out in paragraph 2.1 above shall be increased by INR 1,200 (Rupees One 
thousand two hundred) per month per INR 100,000 (Rupees One hundred thousand) of such 
Security Deposit/ Advance Lease Rent/ Advance License Fee/Premium per Site paid by the PI 
company. 
For the avoidance of doubt, it is clarified that the Rental Threshold Limit for High Deposit Sites shall 
continue to be as per paragraph 2.2.2 of Schedule 3 of the Agreement. 
2.2.3 
The rate so determined, in accordance with paragraphs 2.2.1 and 2.2.2 above, shall be the “Base 
Rate”. Where there is no premium to be added to the Standard Rate for a particular Site, the 
Standard Rate shall be the Base Rate.  
The value of ‘F’ in paragraph 2.2.2 above shall be increased on an annual basis by 2.5% of the 
value of ‘F’ in the previous year of the Service Contract.

In case of upgrading of existing Strategic Site required by the Sharing Operator, the Sharing 
Operator shall pay an additional amount computed in accordance with paragraph 2.3.2 over and 
above the Base Rate mentioned above. 
 
For avoidance of the doubt, for all new Strategic Sites and conversion of Normal Site to Strategic 
Sites, the Base Rate shall be arrived as per paragraph  2.3.2. However, until the Sharing Operator 
and PI company agree to the Base Rate derivation of Strategic Sites as per paragraph  2.3.2, Base 
Rates mentioned in this paragraph 2.3.1 above will be applicable. 
 
2.3.2 
The Base Rate payable by the Sharing Operator in respect of Strategic Sites shall be the sum of 
(i) 20% of the initial capital expenditure incurred by PI company with respect to Passive 
Infrastructure at the Site, calculated on a monthly basis; and (ii) the direct operation and 
maintenance costs incurred by PI company.  A mark-up shall be applicable on each portion, 
where the mark-up shall be determined as follows:  
(i) 
in case there is one operator at the Site: 
a. 7% mark-up on the capital expenditure recovery; and 
b. 20% mark-up on the direct operation and maintenance costs incurred; 
 
(ii) 
in case of two operators at the Site;  
a. 12% mark-up on the capital expenditure recovery; and 
b. 30% mark-up on the direct operation and maintenance costs incurred; and 
 
(iii) 
in case of three or more operators at the Site: 
a. 18% mark-up on the capital expenditure recovery; and 
b. 40% mark-up on the direct operation and maintenance costs incurred. 
 
2.3.3 
Where any other sharing operator wishes to share the Passive Infrastructure at any such Site, 
any upgrade or additional capital expenditure that shall be required to achieve such sharing 
shall be added to the book value of the initial capital expenditure at the time of such upgrade to 
determine the total capital expenditure at the Site.  Upon achieving the sharing, the total capital 
expenditure at the Site shall be equally divided between the operators at the Site in order to 
determine the cost of the capital expenditure to be borne by each operator under paragraph 
2.3.2 above.   
2.3.4 
Capital expenditure on Passive Infrastructure shall include expenditure relating to 
UPS/batteries, poles, racks, installation charges and approval charges, if any.  Other 
expenditures relating to shelters, diesel generators, SMPS etc. shall also be included, if required 
at a particular Site.  If PI Company is required to bear the expenditure for other infrastructure, 
including RF cables, antennae, connectors splitters and planning and installation and 
commissioning cost of such infrastructure, this shall be included in capital expenditure for the 
purposes of paragraph 2.3.2 above.   
2.3.5 
The estimates of capital expenditure and direct operation and maintenance costs at a Site shall 
be mutually agreed between the Parties prior to the Sharing Operator issuing a Service Order to 
PI Company in respect of such Site.  
Direct operation and maintenance costs shall include Site rentals, maintenance costs with 
respect to the Site and the PI Company Infrastructure, security and other miscellaneous

Schedule 5 
Standard Site Access Terms 
1 
Exit Amount 
1.1 
Subject to paragraph 2 below, the amount payable to the PI company by the Sharing Operator 
upon termination of a Service Contract in certain specified cases (the “Exit Amount”) shall be as 
follows:  
(i) 
in relation to termination of Service Contracts with a term of seven (7) years or more, the 
Exit Amount shall be the entire unpaid amount for a period commencing from the date of 
termination until the expiry of seven (7) years from the Charge Effective Date of the 
Service Contract.  
(ii) 
in relation to a Service Contract with a term of less than seven (7) years, the Exit Amount 
shall be the entire unpaid amount for the remainder of the term of such Service Contract. 
For the avoidance of doubt, there shall be no Exit Amount payable post completion of seven (7) 
years from the Charge Effective Date of the Service Contract.  
1.2 
For the avoidance of doubt, while calculating the Exit Amount under paragraph 1.1 above, the 
annual escalation under paragraph 2.5.1 of Schedule 3 (Charges) for the remainder of the term 
shall not be taken into account and the Exit Amount shall be calculated at the Total Rate less 
volume discounts under paragraph 12 of Schedule 3 prevailing at the time of termination.  
1.3 
The consolidation of two or more operators at a Site into a single operator through any merger, 
acquisition, scheme of arrangement etc., shall result in the automatic termination of all service 
contracts in respect of such Site executed by such operators with the PI company except one 
service contract to be determined by such operators at their sole discretion, which shall 
continue to be operational.  In such a case, the relevant exit amount shall be equal to the exit 
amounts determined in respect of the service contract(s) being terminated relating to the Site. 
Where the surviving operator continues to use any equipment of the operator who has ceased 
to exist at the Site, such equipment shall be subject to the loading charges mentioned in 
Schedule 3 (Charges) until such period as the excess loading remains.  For the avoidance of 
doubt, it is hereby clarified that any exit amount pursuant to this paragraph shall be determined 
only after adjusting any free termination entitlement of such operators.   
1.4 
Acquisition of tower business and Merger 
1.4.1 
VIL and VMSL have agreed to sell their tower businesses to the PI company by way of the 
Business Transfer Agreement.  At the Execution Date, a merger of VIL and VMSL with and into 
Idea Cellular Limited (“ICL”) has been announced but not completed (the “Merger”).   
1.4.2 
For the purposes of this paragraph 1.4, the “2014 ICL-Vodafone MSA” is the master services 
agreement dated 7 November 2014 made between ICL, Idea Cellular Infrastructure Services 
Limited, VIL and VMSL, as amended from time to time; and a “Co-located Site” means an 
Existing Site on which, immediately prior to the Effective Date, ICL has active infrastructure 
installed under the terms of a service contract governed by the 2014 ICL-Vodafone MSA. 
1.4.3 
On and from the Effective Date, Active Infrastructure installed at Existing Sites and belonging to

1.4.4 
Notwithstanding paragraph 1.3, the Parties hereby agree that on the date the Merger 
completes:  
(i) 
the Existing Sites Service Contracts relating to the Co-located Sites shall survive and 
remain unaffected; and 
(ii) 
any service contracts ICL has in respect of Co-located Sites shall remain governed by, and 
may be terminated in accordance with, the 2014 ICL-Vodafone MSA and, for the 
avoidance of doubt, without liability for an Exit Amount under the terms of this 
Agreement. 
1.4.5 
If the Merger completes and ICL has terminated a service contract it has in respect of a Co-
located Site under the terms of the 2014 ICL-Vodafone MSA, ICL may elect to keep the relevant 
active infrastructure installed on that Site, and in that circumstance such active infrastructure 
shall be added to the scope of the relevant Existing Sites Service Contract and shall be liable for 
Additional Charges as prescribed by Schedule 3 (Charges) while it remains on the Site.  
1.4.6 
The PI company acknowledges that none of the active infrastructure that remains subject to a 
service contract made under the terms of the 2014 ICL-Vodafone MSA is liable for Additional 
Charges under the terms of this Agreement until such service contract is terminated under the 
terms of the 2014 ICL-Vodafone MSA.  
1.5 
Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement, no exit amount shall be payable by the 
Sharing Operator or Idea Cellular Limited in connection with the termination of service contracts 
as a result of the proposed Merger. For the avoidance of doubt, it is hereby clarified that any 
exit pursuant to the proposed Merger shall not be counted towards the termination entitlement 
of such operators.  
 
2 
Termination of Service Contract 
2.1 
Either Party may terminate a Service Contract by written notice to the other Party at any time 
following without paying any Exit Amount:  
(i) 
a material default, or series of defaults the combination of which is material, by the 
other Party of any of its obligations and failing to remedy such default(s) within 30 
(thirty) days after receipt of written notice giving particulars of the default(s) and 
requiring them to be remedied;  
(ii) 
a change of Law or decision of any Government Authority which necessarily renders the 
existence or performance of the Service Contract void or invalid; or 
(iii) 
due to the lasting of the Force Majeure Event for more than 30 (thirty) days unless 
agreed otherwise in respect of the relevant Site. 
2.2 
In addition, the PI company shall be entitled to terminate a Service Contract in accordance with

the provisions of Clause 6.3 of this Agreement. 
2.3 
Further, the Sharing Operator may terminate a Service Contract pursuant to paragraph 1.17 of 
Schedule 2 of this Agreement without payment of any Exit Amount. 
2.4 
The Sharing Operator shall pay to the PI company the Exit Amount under paragraph 1 above 
upon termination of a Service Contract only in the following cases: 
(i) 
the Sharing Operator has voluntarily terminated such Service Contract;  
(ii) 
such termination is on account of an Insolvency Event in respect of the Sharing 
Operator; or 
(iii) 
such termination is on account of the Sharing Operator being in material default of any 
of its obligations under the Service Contract. 
2.5 
Exit Entitlement 
2.5.1 
Free Exit Entitlement 
Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, during any Financial 
Year, the Sharing Operator shall be entitled to terminate Service Contract(s) between Sharing 
Operator and the PI company, without payment of any Exit Amount, in respect of a number of 
Site(s) equal to 1% of the total number of Sites in respect of which the Sharing Operator has 
entered into Service Contracts with the PI company as of the first day of such Financial Year 
(“Free Exit Entitlement”).  
2.5.2 
Carry Forward of Free Exit Entitlement 
The unutilised portion of the Free Exit Entitlement for a Financial Year shall not lapse at the end 
of that Financial Year, but shall be available to the Sharing Operator to carry forward up to the 
subsequent two (2) Financial Years (“Carry Forward”). E.g., if the Effective Date falls within the 
Financial Year ending on 31 March 2018, the unutilised portion of the Free Exit Entitlement for 
that Financial Year shall be available for Carry Forward up to 31 March 2020, but shall lapse on 1 
April 2020. Similarly, the unutilised portion of the Free Exit Entitlement for the Financial Year 
ending on 31 March 2019 shall be available for Carry Forward up to 31 March 2021, but shall 
lapse on 1 April 2021.  
2.5.3 
Additional Free Exit Entitlement  
After completion of three (3) years from Effective Date, during any Financial Year, the Sharing 
Operator shall be entitled to terminate Service Contract(s) between Sharing Operator and the PI 
company, without payment of any Exit Amount, in respect of a number of Site(s) equal to the 
lower of:  
(i) 
2% of the total number of Sites in respect of which the Sharing Operator has entered 
into Service Contracts with the PI company as of the first day of such Financial Year, and  
(ii) 
2/3rd of the new Service Orders with the PI Company in the same Financial Year (“New 
Service Orders”) 
(“Additional Free Exit Entitlement”).

The Sharing Operator shall remove the Sharing Operator Equipment within 30 (thirty) days of

Sharing Operator Equipment within such period, the Total Rate shall continue to be payable by

3 
Sharing Operators Right to Operate 
Where the PI company, for any reason, is required to cease its operations with respect to any 
Site, the right to operate such Site shall be transferred to an operator at that Site in order to 
maintain the business continuity of the operator(s) at such Site, provided that such operator 
fulfils all the obligations required in relation to the operation of the Site.  In respect of shared 
Sites, the right to operate the Site shall be offered to the operators at that Site in chronological 
order of receipt of their Service Orders in respect of that Site and shall be transferred to the 
operator who accepts such right.  
 
4 
Health and Safety  
The PI company is committed to the health and safety of its employees and other persons who 
have access to any of its Sites.  To ensure the health and safety of all persons at the Sites, the PI 
company shall lay down “Health and Safety Guidelines” governing the activities of all persons 
who shall be permitted access to the Sites.  The Health and Safety Guidelines shall include: 
 
training and information provided to staff and contractors; 
 
information provided to the general public; 
 
provisions relating to site specifications, site design and access control; 
 
provisions relating to operation and maintenance of the sites, including the activities of the 
Sharing Operator; 
 
information provided to landlords; 
 
provisions relating to sites in or on airports, hospitals etc.; and 
 
provisions relating to maintenance of auditable information systems to track the above.  
The Health and Safety Guidelines shall be updated from time to time, and a copy thereof shall 
be provided to the Sharing Operator.

Right to Request Changes 
Within five (5) Business Days after either Party notifies the other of a proposal for a Change, the

2

substantially reflect the details of the Change and shall contain the matters to be agreed 
formally by the Parties in respect of that Change (a “CCN”), within five (5) Business Days after

the Sharing Operator shall only bear an equitable proportion of PI company ’s 
cost; and
